_comment: Public chip/IP catalogue: chip families and their known proven Analogue-Pocket implementations. Every entry is public fact (public GitHub repos + published chip names). Licenses are pointers: confirm each in the linked repo's own LICENSE file. Quality flag is conservative -- only jotego jt51 is a documented true-drop-in exemplar; all others are reference-start because implementations diverge per-instance and must be diffed before reuse.

chips.name: Zilog Z80 (T80 soft-core)

pocket_parts.source: opengateware arcade cores (congo, galaga, gberet, irem_m72, pooyan, xevious), computer-msx

chips.provenance: public repos opengateware/*, agg23/openFPGA-Genesis, ericlewis/*, nullobject/openfpga-tecmo, Mazamars312/*; T80 is Daniel Wallner / MikeJ. Note: T80 diverges across porters (variants differ by hundreds of lines) -- diff before reuse.

chips.name: Zilog Z80 (TV80 soft-core)
chips.provenance: public repos opengateware/arcade-bankp, opengateware/arcade-digdug; TV80 is Guy Hutchison (Verilog).

chips.name: MOS 6502 (T65 soft-core)
chips.provenance: public repos agg23/openfpga-NES, ericlewis/*; T65 is MikeJ (VHDL).

chips.name: Motorola 68000 (fx68k soft-core)
chips.provenance: public repos agg23/openFPGA-Genesis, Mazamars312/*; fx68k is Jorge Cwik. Note: Genesis vs Neogeo copies differ (~64 lines, HALTn wiring) -- diff before reuse.

chips.name: NEC V30 (Irem M72 main CPU family)
chips.provenance: public repos opengateware/arcade-irem_m72, opengateware/arcade-irem_m92; silicon XTAL(32MHz) per mamedev/mame src/mame/irem/m72.cpp.

chips.name: NEC V33/V35 (Irem M92 CPU, carries crypt key)
chips.provenance: public repo opengateware/arcade-irem_m92; note the V35 carries a 256-byte crypt key.

chips.name: Yamaha YM2151 OPM (jotego jt51)

pocket_parts.source: jotego jt-cores (jt51), vendored in opengateware/arcade-irem_m72, opengateware/arcade-irem_m92

chips.provenance: public jotego jt51 library, vendored in public opengateware Irem cores; the documented true-drop-in reuse exemplar.

chips.name: Yamaha YM2612/YM2610/YM2203 (jotego jt12)

pocket_parts.source: jotego jt-cores (jt12), vendored in agg23/openFPGA-Genesis, Mazamars312/Analogue_Pocket_Neogeo

chips.provenance: public jotego jt12 library; note jt03.v differs Genesis vs Neogeo (~45 lines) -- diff before reuse.

chips.name: GI AY-3-8910 / YM2149 (jotego jt49)

pocket_parts.source: jotego jt-cores (jt49), vendored in opengateware/computer-msx, Mazamars312/Analogue_Pocket_Neogeo

chips.provenance: public jotego jt49 library; also a non-jotego ym2149 exists in opengateware/arcade-pooyan.

chips.name: TI SN76489/76496 PSG (jotego jt89 / non-jotego)

pocket_parts.source: jotego jt89 in agg23/openFPGA-Genesis; non-jotego sn76489/96 in opengateware/{arcade-bankp,arcade-congo,arcade-gberet}

chips.provenance: public jotego jt89 + public opengateware cores; the opengateware sn76489/96 is single-porter clustered (best near-identical reuse candidate).

chips.name: Yamaha OPL YM3526/YM3812 (jotego jtopl)
chips.provenance: public jotego jtopl library, vendored in public nullobject/openfpga-tecmo.

pocket_parts.source: jotego jt5205 in nullobject/openfpga-tecmo; non-jotego MSM5205 in agg23/openfpga-pcengine
